mnemonics: AR-MA Sandwich: The AR is the top bread (past values), the MA is the bottom bread (past errors). The 'p' and 'q' are the tasty fillings in between.
keyPoints: An ARMA(p, q) model combines an Autoregressive (AR) component of order p and a Moving Average (MA) component of order q.
The general form is: X_t = c + ε_t + Σ(φ_i * X_{t-i}) + Σ(θ_i * ε_{t-i}) for i=1 to p and j=1 to q.
ARMA models are used for stationary time series and provide a parsimonious description of a more complex process.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for stationarity before fitting an ARMA model, leading to spurious results.
Overfitting the model by choosing orders p and q that are too high, rather than finding the most parsimonious model (e.g., using AIC/BIC).
